title: Question Record
description: JSON-first canonical question record (SPEC §9.4). The single batched discovery round emits an array of these; they render to questions.md for the operator and export straight into the customer-management app. Each resolved one is persisted to ledger/questions/<id>.json. The `id` is a STABLE function of the question's SUBJECT (column/value/entity), never run position, so re-runs and v2 dedupe (SPEC §9.5 idempotency).

id.description: Stable, subject-derived question id. `q_<kind>__<subject-slug>` so the same subject always produces the same id across runs and snapshots (idempotency, SPEC §9.5).

gate.description: Coarse origin of the question within the single discovery round: a mapping ambiguity vs a projected exception. Retained for review grouping (the two old gates collapse into one round, SPEC §9).

kind.description: Question type (SPEC §9.4) — drives how it renders and how the answer is applied. value_set=a coded value did not resolve to a real tenant list_option id, or the value-set meaning is ambiguous; unmapped=a source column feeds a required field but isn't mapped; missing_required=a required target field has no source column; source_key=a table has no stable identifying key; entity_merge=a borderline merge pair; validation=a blocking validation failure data cannot fill.

entity.description: Target Everspot entity the question concerns, if applicable.

field.description: Target field the question concerns, if applicable.

table.description: Source table the question concerns, if applicable.

column.description: Source column the question concerns, if applicable.

question.description: The human-readable prompt rendered into questions.md.

row_refs.description: Pointers to the source location(s) the question is about, e.g. "register!ITYPE" or "BURIALS:48213".

options.description: Allowed answer choices (for value_set, the candidate target tokens/ids; for mapping/source_key, the candidate columns/targets).

allow_custom.description: Whether the operator may supply a value outside `options`.

proposed_answer.description: The AI's suggested answer — MANDATORY so a busy operator can 'accept all'. For value_set, an object source_value->target (or null parts the operator must fill); for source_key, the proposed key columns; for unmapped/missing_required, the proposed source column or null; for validation, the proposed remediation.

confidence.description: AI confidence in the proposed answer.

handoff.description: Who should answer: internal (migration team), client (hand to the customer), either.

status.description: Lifecycle: open (awaiting answer — orchestrator MUST NOT proceed), answered (operator answered), auto-resolved (a confident default was recorded with logging, never silent), skipped (deferred with rationale).

answer.description: The recorded answer (same shape options as proposed_answer). Present when status is answered or auto-resolved.

rationale.description: Why this resolution was reached (the default's basis, or the skip reason).

first_seen_snapshot.description: Snapshot id where this question first arose (e.g. v1).
